facts_authpayload_access_control_1241829.content: Add a comment whenever a role is intentionally excluded from an access check — it makes the design rationale explicit and saves future readers from questioning the exclusion.

conventions_lms_role_model_1241830890546.content: AdvancedContentEditor is the exercise-editor role (manages exercises) and is distinct from course-manager roles — they should not have course-level read access by design.

facts_code_review_3797636122155.content: Adding a clarifying comment is a valid fix when the behavior is correct by design but not self-explanatory to future readers.

thoughts_lmspermissions_3797637550988.content: AdvancedContentEditor is an exercise-editor role, not a course-manager role — intentionally excluded from instructor list read access.

facts_pr_workflow_3797638808744.content: When fixing review feedback, prefer the minimal appropriate solution rather than over-engineering.

conventions_auth_2263530381047.content: Code with surprising exclusions (like AdvancedContentEditor from read access) should include a clarifying comment at the exclusion site explaining the design rationale, not just the intent.

facts_codereview_2263532192762.content: Multi-pass structured review (CRI-Triage → CRI-Detail → CRII-Context → CRIII-Holistic) catches both correctness issues and design-rationale gaps that single-pass reviews miss.

thoughts_auth_2263533838566.content: Without an inline comment at the exclusion site itself, reviewers cannot distinguish intentional design from accidental omission — always comment the 'why' at the decision point.

conventions_general_2263540517110.content: tested with mocks covering all branches including empty/null guards

facts_srcpayloadcollectionscourseinstruc.content: Document intentional security exclusions in comments near the exclusion logic, explaining why access is denied rather than granted — this resolves ambiguity during review.

conventions_srcpayloadaccessinstructorac.content: LMS access control uses a dedicated helper file (instructorAccess.ts) rather than inlining permissions, keeping collection configs clean and reusable.

thoughts_lms_instructor_permissions_2422.content: AdvancedContentEditor must be explicitly excluded from read access in instructor permissions; omitting this exclusion silently grants broader read access than intended.

facts_ui_branching_1372881329486.content: Admin/superuser views often get stubbed with TODO initially — always verify the admin branch returns data, not just instructor views.

conventions_lint_1372883181204.content: Design token warnings in lint are acceptable in this project (not blocking) — only actual errors fail the gate.

facts_auth_routing_1372885506271.content: When roles share a route, branch on role at both API (data) and UI (view) layers to keep concerns separated.

conventions_payload_api_1372887929785.content: Use Payload's `where` clause operators (equals, in) for parameterized queries — never string interpolation.

conventions_i18n_1594293977890.content: Translations must update both messages/en.json and messages/he.json whenever new UI text is added.

conventions_api_design_1594297855202.content: API endpoints branch on isAdmin/role to return filtered (user-scoped) vs. unfiltered (admin) datasets.

facts_data_model_1594302596613.content: Admin dashboard uses courses[].instructors[] array on course entity rather than separate instructor->courses mapping.

facts_authpermissions_4129429319920.content: Using a dedicated `course-instructors` collection (not embedded array) for per-course role assignments enables Payload access control and querying per course.

facts_authpermissions_4129433934303.content: The `primary`/`ta`/`guest` trinary permission model on course-instructors provides sufficient granularity for instructor hierarchy without overcomplicating the role system.

facts_srcserverapi_4129436391274.content: Branching the dashboard API handler on user role (admin vs instructor) keeps the endpoint unified while enabling role-specific logic and data shaping.

thoughts_e2e_testing_4129438911571.content: UI rendering logic must be verified in-browser — heading text (e.g. 'Course Oversight') and conditional rendering depend on role context not always visible in unit tests.

facts_e2e_testing_4129442584871.content: Browser verification of role-dependent UI text catches discrepancies that unit tests miss when mocking auth context.

facts_authpermissions_4213339929040.content: Storing per-course role assignments in a dedicated 'course-instructors' collection (with primary/ta/guest roles) rather than embedding in course documents.

facts_srcserver_4213342609535.content: Branching dashboard API at /api/instructor/dashboard by user role (admin vs. instructor) instead of separate endpoints.

conventions_testing_4213345805805.content: All new features require unit + integration + E2E tests plus browser verification before passing review.
